top10.introHtml: Ranked for the cheap fun second car lens: working top condition first, then reliability, fun factor, and value. Miatas and Solstices dominate for good reason — they're the most sorted drop-tops at this price point.

picks.whyGood: Only 37,018 miles — the lowest-mileage Solstice in this search. West Chester is 9 miles away. 'Great Deal' rated by CarGurus. The Solstice/Sky platform is genuinely fun: rear-wheel drive, 177-hp Ecotec, tight dimensions. Base trim means less electrics to worry about than the turbocharged GXP.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through a full cycle; inspect rear-window seam and headliner for water staining; check rocker panels and rear wheel wells for rust; confirm timing chain tensioner service history (Ecotec L61 is mostly fine but worth confirming); test A/C.
picks.bottomLine: Best mileage-to-price ratio in the whole search on a genuinely fun RWD roadster, 9 miles from Media.

picks.whyGood: ND-generation Miata (2016+) is the best one yet: 155 hp, 2,332 lbs, near-perfect 50/50 balance, and it's the newest platform in this search. 61,200 miles at $14,995 is a 'Great Deal' per CarGurus. Club trim adds a limited-slip differential and sport suspension — exactly what you want for a fun second car.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top operation; look for tears in fabric and rear plastic window yellowing; check for water in footwells (common if drain tubes clogged); inspect for rust on subframe; verify no major accident via Carfax.
picks.bottomLine: The ND Miata Club at this price and mileage is the most compelling all-around fun car in the entire list.

picks.whyGood: NC-generation Miata (2006–2015) with 82k miles — well within the Miata's natural lifespan of 200k+. Touring trim adds Bose audio and power windows. Manual transmission. Sold at a franchised Lexus dealer (likely inspected). Same car also listed on CarGurus at $11,485.
picks.whatToVerify: Confirm no timing chain rattle on cold start; soft top operation and rear window condition; clutch engagement point; rocker panel rust; check for prior damage on Carfax.
picks.bottomLine: A classic NC Miata in driving condition at a fair price — the 'buy a Miata' answer for anyone who can't stretch to the ND.

picks.whyGood: The GXP is the turbocharged Solstice — 260 hp from the 2.0T Ecotec. Clean title and no accidents per Cars.com filters. 'Great Deal' at just $8,195 for 88k miles. The last model year (2009) before GM cancelled the line, so final production quality. NJ is roughly 20–25 minutes from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo boost (check for smoke, listen for wastegate chatter); soft top cycle; check intercooler hose clamps for cracks; rocker-panel rust is the #1 Solstice concern; pull a Carfax for accident history.
picks.bottomLine: 260-hp turbocharged roadster at $8,195 with a clean title — best performance-per-dollar pick in the entire search.

picks.whyGood: E85 Z4 2.5i — 184 hp inline-6, sharp steering, aggressive looks. 70,240 miles is low for a 2005. Devon is only 9 miles away. Premier Eurocars is a BMW/Euro specialist, which matters for pre-purchase confidence. Under $10,100.
picks.whatToVerify: Power soft-top operation (hydraulic — test several full cycles); check for oil leaks at valve cover and coolant hose; VANOS rattle on cold start; inspect convertible top header seal; confirm no rust at rear jack points.
picks.bottomLine: A sharp-looking German roadster at 9 miles away with 70k miles — the BMW choice that's closest to Media.

picks.whyGood: E46 325Ci is one of the most beloved BMW convertibles ever — 189 hp M54 inline-6, balanced chassis, beautiful proportions. Only 60,200 miles for a 2001 is excellent. Same car listed on Cars.com at $10,000 with Good Deal rating. Norristown is 15 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: Top hydraulic pump and cylinder condition (full open/close cycle); coolant expansion tank (E46 common failure point); check rear subframe for cracking at mount points; oil consumption; confirm no rust at strut towers.
picks.bottomLine: The E46 325Ci at 60k miles for $10k is the classic 'enthusiast BMW convertible' — hard to beat for character.

picks.whyGood: 'Great Deal' on a newer-generation BMW convertible — the E88 128i has the N51/N52 inline-6, power retractable hardtop, and modern safety features. 2013 is the last model year, so post any early-generation issues. 69k miles at $9,500 is excellent value.
picks.whatToVerify: Power hardtop mechanism (full cycle — these are more robust than soft tops but seals age); oil leaks at valve cover gasket and oil pan; VANOS; check for rust on the bottom of the doors and rockers; confirm cooling system components replaced.
picks.bottomLine: A proper retractable-hardtop BMW at 'Great Deal' pricing — the most weather-proof convertible on the list.

picks.whyGood: E36/7 Z3 2.8 — the 193-hp M52 is the sweet spot of Z3 engines (more power than 2.3, less complexity than M Roadster). 75k miles, $7,940, 15 miles away. Private-seller pricing means room to negotiate. The Z3 is a proven, simple roadster with a huge enthusiast community.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top condition (fabric and rear window); M52 oil consumption check; VANOS seals; inspect trunk for water intrusion; test A/C; check for rear subframe weld cracks (less common on Z3 than E46 but worth a look).
picks.bottomLine: The cheapest competent roadster in the list — $7,940 for a 2.8 Z3 is a steal if the top is solid.

picks.whyGood: Saturn Sky and Pontiac Solstice share the same Kappa platform — identical fun-factor. Only 39,382 miles is exceptionally low for a 2008. 'Great Deal' rated. The Sky's exterior styling is arguably sharper than the Solstice. Clean title, no accidents via Cars.com search filters.
picks.whatToVerify: Same checklist as Solstice: soft top seams, rocker rust, timing chain; on the Sky also confirm the pop-out side windows work (they can jam); verify A/C operation.
picks.bottomLine: Solstice-level fun at Solstice-level price, with only 39k miles — a seriously low-mileage find.

picks.whyGood: The $7,500 floor-price Solstice — 'Great Deal' on CarGurus and AutoTrader, 63k miles in Telford (27 miles away). Base 177-hp Ecotec is naturally aspirated and very durable. This is the entry-level fun-car pick for someone who wants to spend as little as possible.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top cycle and rear window; rocker rust (early Solstices are most susceptible here); confirm no accident history; oil consumption; A/C.
picks.bottomLine: The best-value drop-top in the search — $7,500 for a 'Great Deal' Solstice with 63k miles.

mustangs.introHtml: All Ford Mustang convertibles found within 35 miles of Media, PA at $7k–$15k. GT trim ranked above V6. SN95 low-mileage examples at the top.

picks.whyGood: SN95 Mustang GT with only 44,000 miles — a genuinely rare low-mileage example. The 5.0 HO V8 (215 hp in '94) is the right engine for a fun second car. Clean title and no accidents per Cars.com. Eddington is close to Media. At $10,500, this compares favorably to higher-mileage examples.
picks.whatToVerify: SN95 convertible-specific checks: inspect the soft top mount-to-rocker seam on both sides for rust perforation (the SN95 is prone here); test power top mechanism; check for rear-axle clunk on takeoff (8.8 axle); inspect T5/T45 transmission for third-gear crunch; look for floor rust under carpet.
picks.bottomLine: 44k-mile SN95 GT is the standout Mustang find — the mileage alone makes it worth a serious look.

picks.whyGood: Another low-mileage SN95 GT — 55,630 miles at a named specialty dealer (GT Motors). Philadelphia pickup at 25 miles. The 1995 GT shares the same 5.0 HO engine. At $10,995 vs the Eddington car's $10,500 it's close — two solid SN95 options to compare.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN95 checklist: top-mount rust, soft top, 8.8 rear axle, T5 third gear; confirm original mileage with service records.
picks.bottomLine: A second SN95 GT worth comparing to the Eddington car — put both on your PPI shortlist.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang GT Premium — the 300-hp 3-valve 4.6L V8. This generation is more refined than the SN95 and the Premium trim adds leather and a Shaker audio system. 'Good Deal' rated at $14,996. Reading is farther out but the S197 GT is the most modern V8 in the Mustang section.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible: check IRS clunk if equipped (verify axle type); power soft top alignment and seal; 4.6 3V Triton cam phaser rattle on cold start; inspect trunk floor for water intrusion; T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test.
picks.bottomLine: The most powerful and modern V8 Mustang in the search — if you want S197 muscle, this is the only GT in range.

picks.whyGood: S197 V6 with the 305-hp Cyclone V6 — nearly as much power as the SN95 GT, better fuel economy, and much more modern (2012). West Chester is only 9 miles away. The V6 Mustangs of this era are underrated fun. 'Fair Deal' at $11,459 — room to negotiate.
picks.whatToVerify: Power soft top mechanism (check all seals and the rear header latch); inspect for water staining under rear seats; 6R80 automatic or MT82 manual — confirm transmission type; check for rear-axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: Modern V6 Mustang 9 miles from Media — the 305-hp 2012 V6 is genuinely quick and the closest Mustang to home.

picks.whyGood: S550 EcoBoost Premium — the newest Mustang in the search. Independent rear suspension, modern tech, 310-hp turbocharged 2.3L. Premium trim adds leather, nav, and MagneRide magnetic dampers. The mileage is high (141k) but the EcoBoost is a durable engine with proper maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: High mileage: verify full service history; retractable soft top hydraulics; check for IRS bushing wear and clunk; EcoBoost intercooler and hose condition; confirm no major accident via Carfax; listen for turbo wastegate rattle.
picks.bottomLine: Newest, most modern Mustang in the list — the mileage is high but the S550 platform is the one worth owning long-term.
